text: Mobile money agency (MTN MoMo, Airtel Money) is one of the easiest businesses to start in Uganda. Requirements: valid national ID, registration with telecom provider (free), SIM card, float capital (minimum UGX 500,000 recommended, UGX 1,000,000+ ideal), a visible location, and a branded kiosk or booth. Commission structure: cash-in (deposit) earns 0.5-1% commission, cash-out (withdrawal) earns the most at 1-2.5% depending on amount. Average daily transactions for a good location: 50-100 transactions. Monthly revenue: UGX 300,000-800,000 in commissions.

Key success factors for mobile money: 1) LOCATION — near markets, taxi parks, hospitals, or schools with high foot traffic. 2) FLOAT MANAGEMENT — always have both e-money and cash available. When you run out of either, you lose customers. 3) EXTENDED HOURS — open early (6:30am) and close late (9pm) to capture workers. 4) TRUST — never shortchange customers, always give receipts. 5) SECURITY — don't keep large amounts of cash; bank daily. 6) DIVERSIFICATION — add airtime sales, data bundles, utility payments to increase traffic.

Mobile money business risks: 1) Robbery — agents handling cash are targets. Mitigate: don't keep excess cash, vary banking times, install CCTV if possible. 2) Fraud — fake IDs, social engineering scams. Always verify IDs, never process suspicious transactions. 3) System downtime — when the network is down, you earn nothing. Have multiple SIM cards (both MTN and Airtel). 4) Float management — running out of e-float or cash means turning away customers. Track your balance hourly. 5) Competition — many agents in small areas. Differentiate with better service and longer hours.
